prompt: A highly detailed cinematic interior of a cramped Victorian steampunk ship cabin engine room, viewed from standing eye level in a narrow corridor. The scene is packed wall-to-wall with aged polished brass pipes, copper tubes, riveted metal tanks, valves, gauges, and dark varnished wooden cabinets. On the left foreground, a steep wooden staircase with curved banister enters the frame diagonally. Along both sides of the corridor are heavy antique drawers and machinery consoles covered with small metal instruments, teapots, kettles, and mechanical fittings. At the back center is a small square-paned porthole window with 6 visible panes, showing a pale overcast exterior and a faint distant industrial skyline. Beneath the window is a narrow shelf unit with 3 open levels and a small drawer. On the right wall near the window hangs 1 round analog clock. The walls are painted teal-green metal with peeling paint and weathered patina. The floor is dark wood with a narrow worn patterned rug runner. Lighting is warm and moody, primarily from 1 glowing amber industrial wall lamp near the upper left of center, mixed with soft cold daylight from the porthole. Emphasize rich material texture, tarnished brass, oxidized copper, chipped paint, dust, age, and a lived-in mechanical atmosphere. Symmetrical depth, dense environmental storytelling, ultra-detailed, realistic, cinematic composition, shallow contrast but sharp focus throughout, no people.
